name: vmarkd
description: vMarkd — a full-featured visual markdown editor.
author: Sławomir Piochacz

untrustedWorkspaces.description: vMarkd renders and edits markdown, but defers writing images and creating wiki pages until the workspace is trusted.

virtualWorkspaces.description: Editing works; features that depend on the local filesystem (image upload, wiki page creation, asset resolution) are unavailable in virtual workspaces.

commands.title: Open with vMarkd to the side

editor/title.when: !isInDiffEditor && activeCustomEditorId != vmarkd.editor && (resourceExtname == .md || resourceExtname == .markdown) && (resourceScheme == file || resourceScheme == untitled)
!isInDiffEditor && activeCustomEditorId != vmarkd.editor && (resourceExtname == .md || resourceExtname == .markdown) && (resourceScheme == file || resourceScheme == untitled)

vmarkd.theme.useVscodeColors.description: Apply the VS Code theme's colors to the markdown editor.

enumDescriptions: Follow the VS Code light/dark theme — github in a light theme, github-dark in a dark one.
vmarkd.theme.code.description: Syntax-highlight theme for code blocks (highlight.js). "auto" follows the VS Code light/dark theme (github / github-dark); pick any of the 73 highlight.js styles to override. Applies live.

enumDescriptions: Follow the VS Code theme — dark palette in a dark theme (renders a plain flowchart mostly monochrome).
Force the dark palette regardless of the editor theme.
vmarkd.theme.mermaid.description: Theme for Mermaid diagrams. "auto" follows the editor (dark in a dark theme) — which renders a plain flowchart mostly monochrome; pick "default"/"forest"/"neutral" for colour, or "dark" to force the dark palette.

vmarkd.editor.fullWidth.description: Enable full-width layout for the editor (removes max-width centering).

vmarkd.editor.fontSize.description: Base font size of the editor content. "editor" follows VS Code's editor font size (recommended), "vditor" keeps Vditor's larger 16px reading size, or enter a number for an explicit pixel size (e.g. "15"). Headings scale proportionally.

vmarkd.theme.highlightHeadings.description: Apply a themed background to headings (h1–h6) for easier scanning.

vmarkd.editor.headingMarkers.description: Show the IR-mode left-gutter markers (heading levels H1–H6, the link-reference ↩, etc.). Turn off to hide them and tighten the left margin.

vmarkd.editor.codeLineNumbers.description: Show line numbers in fenced code blocks (rendered preview). Off by default — adds a line-number gutter per code block.

vmarkd.editor.toolbar.description: Show the editor toolbar. When off, the editor opens without a toolbar.

vmarkd.editor.linkOpenWithModifier.description: Follow links with Ctrl+click (Cmd+click on macOS); a plain click places the caret in the link for editing. Turn off to open links on a plain click instead.

vmarkd.css.external.description: Paths to external CSS files (absolute, or relative to the workspace root) injected into the editor. They reload live when edited. customCss is loaded after them, so it always wins on conflicting rules. Resource-scoped — a workspace/folder can override it in `.vscode/settings.json`.

vmarkd.outline.openByDefault.description: Open the outline panel automatically when an editor opens.

vmarkd.outline.position.description: Which side the outline panel opens on.

vmarkd.outline.width.description: Width of the outline panel, in pixels.

vmarkd.outline.highlight.description: Briefly flash a heading when you click it in the outline.

vmarkd.advanced.instantPreview.description: Instant preview: before the live editor finishes loading, paint a fast read-only preview of the document and a placeholder toolbar so the file appears immediately, then swap to the live editor seamlessly. Turn off to open straight into the live editor with no pre-render.

vmarkd.advanced.streamLargeFiles.description: Stream large markdown files into the editor in chunks instead of one blocking render (which can freeze the editor for seconds on big files). While streaming, the editor is briefly read-only with a loading spinner. Turn off to render large files in a single pass.

vmarkd.advanced.retainHidden.description: Keep hidden editor tabs alive for instant switching. Turn off to free memory when many tabs are open (each hidden editor is fully retained), at the cost of a reload when you switch back. Changing this requires reloading the window.

vmarkd.image.saveFolder.description: Save images to which folder when upload in markdown, default relative to the markdown file, you can set to project root via `${projectRoot}/assets`. Resource-scoped — a workspace/folder can override it in `.vscode/settings.json`.
